data.title: Sanara MedTech outlines 2026 net revenue guidance of $116M–$121M while expanding distributor network and product reach
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Sanara MedTech Inc. (SMTI) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Seth Yon reported that Sanara MedTech surpassed $100 million in net revenue for the first time, achieving $103.1 million for full year 2025, representing 19% year-over-year growth. Yon noted, “Our performance demonstrates the strength of our hybrid commercial model, which includes both field sales reps and a growing network of independent distributor partners.”
  • Yon emphasized an expansion in the distributor network to over 450 contracted partners by year-end, up from more than 350 in 2024, and highlighted success in selling into over 1,450 healthcare facilities, compared to 1,300 the previous year.
  • The CEO outlined strategic progress, including a completed wind-down of the Tissue Health Plus (THP) segment, and cited a significant Vizient contract for BIASURGE, which provides access to approximately 1,800 healthcare facilities.
  • Yon stated, “We are entering 2026 as a focused pure-play surgical company dedicated exclusively to the operating room setting with three anchor products, two currently in the market, CellerateRx Surgical and BIASURGE and one in our pipeline, OsStic.”
  • CFO Elizabeth Taylor reported, “Fourth quarter gross profit increased $1.6 million or 7% to $25.7 million. Fourth quarter gross margin increased approximately 175 basis points to 93% of net revenue, driven primarily by sales of soft tissue repair products and lower manufacturing costs related to CellerateRx Surgical.”

Outlook

  • Taylor reaffirmed full-year 2026 net revenue guidance at $116 million to $121 million, representing growth of approximately 13% to 17% over 2025 levels.
  • Management expects first quarter 2026 net revenue of approximately $26.7 million to $27.2 million, translating to 14% to 16% year-over-year growth.
  • Yon stated the company will invest in expanding the field sales team and R&D to “lay the foundation for strong, sustainable growth in 2026 and the coming years.”

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter net revenue was $27.5 million, up 5% year-over-year, with a 13% growth rate when excluding the prior year’s $1.8 million BIASURGE sales from Hurricane Helene disruption.
  • Operating expenses increased $2.8 million to $24.6 million, including a $1.8 million noncash impairment charge and a $1.2 million increase in R&D. Adjusted EBITDA was $4.7 million for the quarter.
  • Net loss from continuing operations was $1.1 million, or ($0.13) per diluted share. Cash provided by operations for the full year was $6.8 million, compared to $24,000 used in 2024.
  • The company ended the year with $16.6 million in cash and $46 million in long-term debt.

Q&A

  • Eduardo Martinez-Montes, H.C. Wainwright: “Following the Vizient contract effective January 1, how much of your growth in 2026 do you think is attributable to this new volume of GPO versus organic growth in existing accounts? And do you anticipate any other of these deals to materialize in 2026?” CEO Yon replied, “It will still take a little bit of time to go out and educate at the facility level... now to have on contract 1,800-plus facilities, we think that gives us great runway to perform in 2026, but truly well beyond that as well.”
  • Martinez-Montes also asked about CellerateRX growth and the impact of new clinical studies. Yon responded, “We feel really confident in that economic study that came out. We think that facilities will see great value in that as well to showcase a product that, again, is a supply cost inside the DRG... So I think it only strengthens our relationships inside the hospital.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts conveyed a positive and inquisitive tone, focusing on growth prospects following the Vizient contract and the clinical evidence supporting product adoption.
  • Management’s tone was confident in prepared remarks, highlighting phrases such as “We feel really confident” and “we think that gives us great runway.” During Q&A, the tone remained constructive and forward-looking, with no defensive or hesitant language observed.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management maintained a positive sentiment, shifting from a focus on winding down THP to opportunities for expansion in the surgical market.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter saw the introduction and reaffirmation of explicit full-year 2026 net revenue guidance, whereas the previous quarter did not provide this level of outlook.
  • There was a continued strategic shift, with the THP segment now fully wound down, sharpening focus on the surgical business and core products.
  • Revenue growth decelerated to single digits in the quarter, compared to 22% year-over-year growth reported in the third quarter; however, the company highlighted normalized double-digit growth when adjusting for the previous year’s BIASURGE spike.
  • Management’s confidence in operational leverage and expansion of the distributor network was emphasized more strongly than in the previous quarter.
  • Analysts in both quarters focused on revenue growth cadence, margin trends, and the impact of strategic decisions, but current questions centered on the scale-up potential from the Vizient contract and clinical evidence for product adoption.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited a noncash impairment charge related to the strategic shift, higher R&D expenses, and increased interest costs.
  • The company highlighted the need for education and onboarding at newly contracted Vizient facilities, recognizing that growth will depend on product adoption at the facility level.
  • No material cash spend is anticipated for THP going forward, reducing one prior area of concern.

Final Takeaway

Sanara MedTech is entering 2026 as a focused pure-play surgical company, reaffirming its net revenue guidance of $116 million to $121 million and expecting strong growth from its expanded distributor network and new Vizient contract for BIASURGE. Management is confident in the company’s ability to deliver sustainable growth through targeted investments in field sales and R&D while maintaining strong margins and operational leverage. The transition away from the THP segment and advances in clinical evidence for anchor products are expected to support the next phase of value creation for shareholders.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Concentrix targets $630M-$650M in adjusted free cash flow for 2026 as AI pipeline expands
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Concentrix Corporation (CNXC) Q1 2026

Management View

  • President and CEO Christopher Caldwell indicated that "we continue to win the right business, drive the right revenue mix and execute on our strategy, allowing us to come within our guide for both revenue and profit." Caldwell highlighted a "61% year-over-year" increase in technology-driven wins and that "our signed annual contract value for solutions, including AI, more than doubled" quarter-on-quarter. Caldwell cited closing "close to 60 enterprise iX suite deals in the quarter including our largest iX Hero contracts to date with 2 Fortune 50 companies," emphasizing proprietary AI technologies and strategic focus on high-value services and client consolidation.
  • CFO Andre Valentine reported, "We delivered revenue of approximately $2.5 billion, an increase of 1.9% on a constant currency basis and over 5% on a reported basis." Valentine explained, "Our non-GAAP operating income was $295 million. The midpoint of the guidance range we provided on our last call... Adjusted EBITDA in the quarter was $348 million, a margin of 13.9%. Non-GAAP diluted EPS was $2.61 in line with the guidance range we provided in January." He noted a $6 million GAAP loss on the sale of two nonstrategic businesses, generating about $20 million in proceeds, and referenced cost actions expected to yield "approximately $40 million in annualized savings over and above investments in growth."

Outlook

  • Valentine stated, "For the second quarter, we expect the following: second quarter revenue of $2.46 billion to $2.485 billion... The guidance implies constant currency revenue growth for the quarter, ranging from 1% to 2%." He projected second quarter non-GAAP operating income of $290 million to $300 million, and "second quarter non-GAAP earnings per share will be expected to be $2.57 to $2.69 per share." Full-year guidance for non-GAAP metrics remains unchanged, including "between $630 million and $650 million in adjusted free cash flow." The company expects "aggregate proceeds for approximately $40 million from asset sales" in 2026 and aims to reduce net leverage to below 2.6x adjusted EBITDA by the end of the year.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q1 2026 was reported at $2.5 billion, with a 1.9% increase on a constant currency basis. Banking and financial services clients delivered "13% year-over-year" growth, while retail, travel, and e-commerce grew 6%, and media and communications 3%. Technology and consumer electronics, and healthcare verticals decreased "about 6% driven by lighter volumes than clients expected and shore mix."
  • Adjusted free cash flow was negative $145 million for the quarter, attributed to seasonal factors and timing of cash receipts, with all related receivables "collected in the first week of March." The company returned $65 million to shareholders, including $42 million in share repurchases and $23 million in dividends. At quarter-end, cash and equivalents stood at $234 million, total debt at $4.75 billion, and net debt at $4.51 billion. Liquidity was nearly $1.4 billion.

Q&A

  • Ruplu Bhattacharya, BofA Securities: Asked about AI and iX suite revenue and pricing, and AI investments for 2026. Caldwell explained that "our iX Hello solution... is priced by consumption," and the Hero product is "a subscription basis," projecting to be "at or above $100 million [ARR] by the end of this fiscal year." Caldwell said they are "a little ahead of plan from where we expected based on what we've sold within the first quarter."
  • Bhattacharya queried margin progression and improvement cadence. Valentine responded, "The guidance is very much consistent with what we said entering the year, which was we thought that margins would be somewhat compressed in the first half, and then we would see sequential margin expansion in the second half of the year."
  • Bhattacharya asked about offshore movement and customer volumes. Valentine stated, "the trend towards moving work offshore continues," accounting for a "2-point headwind from shore movement." He added that prior excess capacity and volume issues are "pretty much playing out in line with our expectation."
  • Lucas Morison, Canaccord Genuity: Inquired about portfolio divestitures. Valentine replied, "We're not really looking to shed anything else at this point in time... These 2 businesses were quite small, not strategic, not growing, not accretive to overall margins."
  • Morison also asked about the two declining verticals. Valentine clarified, "health care... saw lighter volumes than expected, largely related to changes in Medicare membership... we don't see that really returning to growth here for a couple of quarters." For tech and consumer electronics, "underlying volumes are down, a little bit of impact of automation... volatility... could go up or down as we go through the second half of 2026."
  • David Koning, Baird: Queried long-term margin potential. Caldwell responded, "our expectation is we continue to build on that as we get some of these other programs up to scale as we put in the new AI... our expectations is we get this back to historical margins and then we can progress past there." Koning also asked about sustainability of growth in banking and retail. Caldwell said, "banking... we expect there to be fairly strong strength through the course of the year... we're seeing broad-based success."
  • Vincent Colicchio, Barrington Research: Asked about geopolitical issues and excess capacity impact. Caldwell stated, "Our exposure to this is about 1% of revenue... so far, we haven't seen sort of an impact at this point in time." Valentine said excess capacity impacted margin by "20 to 40 basis point range."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for clarity on AI revenue, margin trajectory, and vertical stabilization, showing a neutral to slightly positive tone as they sought confirmation of management’s confidence and details behind guidance.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks, emphasizing successful execution, strong AI adoption, and cost control. In Q&A, management continued with detailed, transparent responses, addressing concerns without defensive language, and reiterated confidence using phrases like "we are confident" and "we have a great deal of confidence."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s sentiment remains consistently confident, with both periods highlighting margin recovery and AI-driven growth, though the current quarter includes more explicit references to cost-saving actions and portfolio optimization.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for Q2 2026 revenue and EPS closely aligns with previous quarter's outlook, with no changes to full-year targets. Year-over-year, constant currency revenue growth slowed to 1.9% from over 3% in Q4, while vertical growth in banking accelerated and technology/healthcare verticals further declined.
  • The strategic focus remains on complex, high-value solutions, AI integration, and efficiency. Management continues to target margin expansion in the second half of the year, with explicit cost-saving initiatives and selective portfolio divestitures surfacing this quarter.
  • Analysts' focus shifted slightly from questions about expansion and investment in AI (Q4) to more granular questions on margin recovery timing and the sustainability of vertical performance (Q1).
  • Management’s confidence level and communication style remains steady, emphasizing stable execution, but with additional details on cost actions and portfolio pruning.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing margin headwinds due to initial implementation of high-value solutions and continued shift of work offshore.
  • Healthcare and technology verticals face ongoing volume challenges, with healthcare not expected to recover for "a couple of quarters."
  • Excess capacity remains a short-term drag on margins, but is expected to improve as business scales through 2026.
  • Management acknowledged geopolitical risks but stated exposure is "about 1% of revenue," with no material impact observed so far. Portfolio divestitures were attributed to non-strategic, non-accretive businesses, with no immediate plans for further sales.

Final Takeaway

Concentrix reinforced its strategy of pursuing high-value, technology-driven solutions and expanding its proprietary AI offerings, with record contract value growth and strong client adoption. Management reiterated unchanged full-year guidance, highlighted proactive cost management and portfolio streamlining, and projected sequential margin improvement through the year. The company remains focused on sustained free cash flow generation and leverage reduction, supported by continuing demand for complex, high-margin services and expanding AI-driven opportunities across key verticals.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Ultragenyx downgraded to neutral at Goldman Sachs on setrusumab data
data.description:
Goldman Sachs has downgraded Ultragenyx Pharmaceutical (RARE) to neutral from buy citing disappointing phase 3 trial results released in December for seralutinib as a treatment for the rare genetic bone disorder osteogenesis imperfecta.

The bank has a $25 price target (~23% upside based on March 23 close).

Analyst Salveen Richter noted that the data from the two trials missed statistical significance on annualized fracture rates vs. placebo or standard-of-care bisphosphonates, the primary endpoint. However, a secondary endpoint, bone mineral density, was met with statistical significance in both studies.

Although Ultragenyx is conducting additional analyses on the results to determine next steps forward, "we see a limited regulatory/commercial path forward for setrusumab, thus removing the key near-term growth lever for the Company."

Richter said that Angelman syndrome asset GTX-102 is now the key value driver, "albeit where we see risk to Ph3 data in 2H given the trial design and disease heterogeneity, as well as competitive dynamics per IONS’ mechanistically similar ION582."

And while achieving GAAP profitability in 2027 is possible, it will need "the 2H+ approvals/launches of gene therapies DTX401 in [glycogen storage disease type Ia (GSD1a)]  and UX111 in [Sanfilippo syndrome (MPS IIIA), which could unlock two priority review vouchers (currently valued at ~$150mn each in our model)."

data.title: GameStop options lean bullish ahead of earnings
data.description:
With no analyst consensus to anchor expectations, the options market is the only price discovery mechanism available going into GameStop's (GME) Q4 report—and it's telling a story that goes well beyond the quarterly numbers.

GameStop shares closed at $23.03 on Monday, with the March 27 options chain pricing in a move of roughly 7-8% in either direction by Friday's expiration. Without enough analysts covering the stock to generate consensus estimates, there is no beat or miss to anticipate in the conventional sense. The earnings reaction will be driven by what CEO Ryan Cohen says, or doesn't say, about his ambitions to transform GameStop into a Berkshire Hathaway-style acquisition platform.

The options lean is bullish — but the $25 calls tell a specific story

The call side dominates the chain with clear conviction. The $25 call is the single most active strike in the entire chain, with 9,355 contracts traded and 10,374 in open interest — sitting roughly 8.5% above Monday's close and comfortably outside the implied move range. The $23.50 and $24 calls also saw heavy activity, with 7,517 and 5,417 contracts respectively.

Put activity is comparatively subdued. The $22 put saw 4,478 contracts and the $22.50 put 4,168 — meaningful but clearly secondary to the bullish positioning above the current price.

The concentration at the $25 call takes on added significance given Cohen's recently stated M&A ambitions. Traders buying those calls may be less interested in the quarterly figures than in any color the report provides on deal timing, financing, and potential targets.

The $30 calls: betting the company

Even more striking is the open interest at the $30 call — 10,668 contracts at a strike roughly 30% above the current stock price. Positioned well beyond any reasonable earnings-driven move, these look less like a short-term earnings play and more like a longer-term bet on Cohen's acquisition strategy paying off dramatically.

That framing is reinforced by Cohen's incentive package, which pays out only if GameStop reaches at least $20 billion in market cap and $2B in cumulative EBITDA, with full vesting tied to a $100B market cap and $10B in cumulative EBITDA. At Monday's close, GameStop's market cap stood at $10.1B. The $30 calls are not an earnings trade — they are a bet that Cohen lands a transformative deal.

The meme factor

With short interest at 14.7% of the float, GameStop retains meaningful short squeeze potential should sentiment turn sharply positive. The options chain reflects both genuine strategic positioning and the speculative meme energy that has historically caused the stock to move violently and unpredictably. Those forces could amplify any post-earnings move well beyond the 7-8% the options market is currently implying.

data.title: HP reveals new high-performance PCs able to support up to four Nvidia Blackwell GPUs
data.description:
HP (HPQ) revealed a new line-up of high-performance PCs capable of supporting up to four of Nvidia's (NVDA) RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell Max-Q Workstation Edition GPUs today at its Imagine 2026 conference.

The HP Z8 Fury G6i workstation is built for high-performance computing, AI development, visual effects, and simulation workloads. With the HP ZBoost, it can also share GPU resources with other computers.

"HP Z workstations are built to equip the best and brightest professionals with the tools they need for specialized workflows and AI at the edge, while giving IT decision makers the ability to scale performance responsibly," said Jim Nottingham, SVP and Division President of HP's Advanced Compute Solutions. 

HP also released what it calls mobile workstations with the HP ZBook X G2i, HP ZBook 8 G2i, and ZBook 8G2a. The HP ZBook X is the world's most powerful 16" mainstream mobile workstation and features powerful 3000-level graphics and 128GB RAM, according to HP. 

HP collaborated with Intel (INTC) and Autodesk (ADSK) to enable architects and engineers to run complex, multi-software workflows wherever required on the new devices.

The new workstations and laptops are expected to be available in April. Prices will be revealed closer to the release date.

data.title: Brent could jump to $120/bbl over the coming weeks – Citi
data.description:
Brent crude oil (CO1:COM) is expected to climb to $120 per barrel over the next few weeks as tensions between Iran and Israel continue to roil energy markets, according to Max Layton, Citi’s global head of commodities research.

The commodity is currently trading around $100, representing a significant potential jump as the conflict shows few signs of abating. The rally in oil prices has already delivered a substantial blow to the global economy.

In an interview with CNBC, Layton explained that the cost of crude oil and petroleum products has surged from approximately $2.5T at the start of the year to an annualized $4.5T—a shock equivalent to 2% of global GDP.

“The two sides are still very, very far apart,” Layton said, noting that while negotiations may be occurring, it remains difficult to see how demands from both Iran and Israel can be reconciled in the short term.

In a worst-case scenario, oil prices (CL1:COM), (CO1:COM) could spike dramatically higher if disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz persist.

Layton outlined a “bull case” where Brent (CO1:COM) reaches $150 per barrel, with total prices including product premiums climbing to $200 or more—levels not seen since the 2008 spike. If the conflict continues for two to three months, the economic damage will rival the combined impact of both 1970s oil shocks, resulting in a devastating 6% to 7% hit to global GDP.

The best-case scenario, however, would see a rapid de-escalation and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. Under this outcome, Layton expects prices to fall back to the $70 to $80 range relatively quickly. He characterized a loss of 200 to 300 million barrels of production as “manageable” if a resolution comes within the week.

Citi’s baseline view remains that both sides will eventually step back from the brink after experiencing the severe economic and military costs of prolonged conflict.

“The baseline view is that seeing that more fully and starting to feel that over the next four weeks results in the resolution,” Layton said. By year’s end, he expects prices to return to approximately $80 per barrel, roughly in line with current forward pricing.

Layton noted that while current volume losses actually exceed those experienced during the 1970s oil crises, the critical variable is duration. The 1970s oil shocks lasted five to six months, whereas Citi anticipates this crisis will be resolved in four to six weeks as both sides fully comprehend the consequences of continued escalation.

Oil ETFs: (USO), (UCO), (DBO), (OILK), and (USL).

Natural Gas ETFs: (UNG), (BOIL), and (UNL).

data.title: $14T debt wave looms, threatening higher rates and wider spreads, Apollo warns
data.description:
A surge in high-quality debt issuance is expected to test investor demand and keep upward pressure on borrowing costs, according to Apollo’s chief economist Torsten Slok.

In total, roughly $14T in investment-grade supply could come to market over the next year, driven by a combination of public and private sector financing needs. 

A significant portion stems from the U.S. government, which must refinance approximately $10T in existing debt over the next 12 months while also funding an estimated $2T budget deficit. 

At the same time, corporate borrowers are poised to add to the supply wave, with gross investment-grade issuance projected to reach around $2T in 2026. Increased borrowing from large technology firms, particularly hyperscalers, is expected to play a key role. 

Taken together, the influx of new debt is likely to create a more competitive environment for capital, putting upward pressure on Treasury yields and widening credit spreads as investors demand higher compensation to absorb the growing supply. 

“The bottom line is that the growing supply of investment grade fixed income product is putting upward pressure on rates and credit spreads,” Slok said. 

U.S. Treasury Yields: (US2Y), (US5Y), (US10Y), (US20Y), and (US30Y).

Fixed Income ETFs: (TLT), (TLH), (IEF), (IEI), (SHY), (SGOV), (SCHO), (BIL), (AGG), (BND), (VCIT), (MUB), (MBB), (JNK), (LQD), (HYG), (VTIP), (TIP), (SCHP), (STIP), (TIPX), (SPIP), (WIP), (GTIP), (LQDI), and (RINF).

data.title: ImmunityBio falls as FDA issues warning over Anktiva ad, podcast
data.description:
ImmunityBio (IBRX) shares dropped ~21% in the morning hours on Tuesday after the U.S. FDA issued a warning letter regarding a TV advertisement and a podcast that it alleged spread false and misleading information related to the company’s bladder cancer drug Anktiva.

In the letter to ImmunityBio (IBRX) CEO Richard Adcock, the regulator said that its Office of Prescription Drug Promotion has determined that the TV commercial and podcast titled "Is the FDA BLOCKING Life Saving Cancer Treatments?” violated federal law.

The promotional materials misbrand Anktiva and make the distribution of the immunotherapy in violation of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act, the FDA said. The agency added that the TV ad and podcast also falsely suggest that the drug is approved for new indications for which the company has yet to receive U.S. approval.

Anktiva is available in the U.S. only with Bacillus Calmette-Guérin ((BCG)) therapy for adults with BCG-unresponsive non-muscle invasive bladder cancer ((NMIBC)) with carcinoma in situ, with or without papillary tumors.

The FDA required ImmunityBio (IBRX) to submit a written response to the letter within 15 working days and threatened to take legal action if the company failed to take sufficient measures to address the violations.

data.title: Zoox adds Austin and Miami to its robotaxi testing ground
data.description:
Zoox (ZOOX) announced on Tuesday that it is close to launching robotaxi service in Austin and Miami on a limited basis to employees and their families as a final step before opening up the service to the public. 

Currently, Zoox (ZOOX) is offering free driverless rides around the Las Vegas Strip and certain San Francisco neighborhoods while also testing in 8 other cities. As part of a measured rollout, Zoox (ZOOX) will have 100 robotaxis on public roads once its service scales in Las Vegas and San Francisco, and it begins testing in Austin and Miami.

Zoox (ZOOX) showcased its robotaxi in Austin earlier this month during the South by Southwest Conference and Festival. As part of the initial launch, Zoox (ZOOX) robotaxis will operate in popular ride-hailing neighborhoods, although the precise service area was not formally articulated.

Notably, Zoox (ZOOX) disclosed that its robotaxis have driven nearly two million autonomous miles and carried over 350K riders to date.

Zoox (ZOOX) stands out from Tesla (TSLA) and Waymo (WAYMO) because its robotaxi is more of an electric, bidirectional, driverless shuttle designed specifically for on-demand urban ride-hailing rather than private ownership. It has a compact, pod-like body with no front or rear, four-wheel steering, and roof-mounted sensor towers at each corner that provide overlapping 360-degree vision and long-range perception. Sliding doors on each side open to a flat, low floor and an interior with four carriage-style seats facing one another in a symmetric "living-room-like" cabin. 

 

 

 

As expected with a true robotaxi, there is no steering wheel or driver's seat; instead, each passenger gets an equivalent seat with their screen and controls for climate, media, and lighting. The autonomous system combines data from cameras, lidar, radar, and infrared to perceive its surroundings, predict what other road users will do, and plan a path for Level 4 driverless operation in mapped urban zones.

data.title: Markets could take some time to recover even with an Iran deal possibly coming soon – Julius Baer
data.description:
Mark Matthews, head of research for Asia at Julius Baer, dismissed President Trump’s Friday deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz as meaningless, noting that previous deadlines have already come and gone without consequence.

Despite the geopolitical uncertainty dragging down U.S. stocks (SP500), (DJI), (COMP:IND), Matthews expressed optimism that a diplomatic resolution could emerge soon.

“The deadline was supposed to be this morning, and it didn’t happen. So, I don’t think that Friday deadline means anything,” Matthews said in an interview with CNBC.

However, he cautioned investors against expecting an immediate market recovery, suggesting that equities face fundamental headwinds regardless of any Iran deal.

The strategist warned against what he called “magical thinking” about markets bouncing back quickly, pointing to technical factors pulling stocks lower. He noted that the S&P 500 (SP500) requires at least a 10% correction to satisfy technical analysts, while the index has only declined about 6% from its peak.

“I have a kind of conflicting message for you because I do think the prospect for a deal by early March is possible, but I also think that the stock market should go down more,” Matthews explained.

Matthews expressed particular concern about concentration in U.S. technology stocks (VGT), (XLK), (IYW), noting that eight of the ten largest companies globally are now American tech firms compared to just two a decade ago.

He highlighted that rising capital expenditures among hyperscalers have caused their “free cash flow to literally collapse as a percent of their income,” limiting their ability to fund buybacks and dividends.

Rather than moving to cash, Julius Baer is advising high-net-worth clients to rotate their portfolios toward other regions, particularly China and Hong Kong.

Matthews cited improved economic decision-making in China and a “slow and steady appreciation” of the renminbi as tailwinds, adding that Hong Kong landlords are trading at “discounts to their net asset value of over 50%” even as physical property prices rise.

Looking ahead, the bank forecasts that crude oil prices (CL1:COM) will stabilize in the high sixties within three months.

While acknowledging that Asia remains vulnerable to energy disruptions, Matthews maintained that the region offers “the best potential” for equity growth over the coming years once the current geopolitical crisis subsides.
